## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On February 24, 1868, the United States House of Representatives took a massive, unprecedented constitutional action by officially voting to impeach President Andrew Johnson. The massive political crisis was heavily triggered by Johnson's aggressive violation of the Tenure of Office Act and his massive, stubborn resistance to the Radical Republicans' massive reconstruction policies in the post-Civil War South. He was ultimately acquitted in the Senate by a massive, razor-thin margin of just a single vote.

On February 24, 1582, Pope Gregory XIII issued a massive, highly historic papal bull titled *Inter gravissimas*. This massive decree officially established the Gregorian calendar, aggressively replacing the heavily flawed, massive Julian calendar that had been in use since the Roman Empire. The massive, mathematically precise adjustment heavily corrected the drift of the solar year, fundamentally standardizing the massive measurement of time across the globe for centuries to come.

She served as the deeply revered, yet highly controversial Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu for over fourteen years, heavily defining the massive, populist intersection of cinema and politics that aggressively dominated southern India.
